whatItDoes: Calculates estimated travel time based on distance and speed. Supports both metric (km/km/h) and imperial (miles/mph) units with an instant swap toggle. Outputs travel duration in hours, minutes, and seconds, along with unit conversions and an estimated arrival time based on the current clock.
whyItExists: Quickly estimating how long a trip will take is a common need for travelers, logistics planners, and anyone doing route calculations. This tool provides instant results with flexible unit support without needing a full mapping application.
whoShouldUse: Travelers planning trip durations, logistics coordinators estimating delivery times, students learning about speed-distance-time relationships, and developers building travel-related features.
useCases: Estimate driving time for a road trip by entering the route distance and average speed
Calculate delivery time for logistics based on distance and vehicle speed
Convert between km/h and mph while computing travel duration
Plan commute times by entering distance and expected average speed
Teach speed-distance-time physics concepts with real-time interactive calculation
instructions: Enter the travel distance in the Distance field
Select the distance unit: kilometers (km) or miles (mi)
Enter the average speed in the Speed field
Select the speed unit: km/h or mph
Click "Swap Units" to toggle between metric and imperial
Read the estimated travel time and unit conversions in the results section
examples: Distance: 100 km, Speed: 100 km/h -> Travel time: 1h 0m 0s
Distance: 26.2 miles, Speed: 6 mph -> Travel time: 4h 22m 0s (marathon pace)
bestPractices: Use average speed including stops and traffic for realistic estimates, not just highway speed
Account for acceleration/deceleration by using a speed 10-15% lower than the posted limit
Use the swap feature to quickly convert between metric and imperial for international trips
The arrival time is based on starting now — adjust if your actual start time differs
For multi-leg journeys, calculate each segment separately and sum the times
commonMistakes: Using the maximum speed limit as the average speed — real average is usually lower
Forgetting to convert units when distance is in km but speed is in mph or vice versa
Entering zero or negative speed, which produces invalid results
Assuming the calculation accounts for traffic, stops, or route changes — it uses constant speed only
faq: Does this account for traffic or stops? No — it assumes constant speed. Use a lower average speed to account for real-world conditions.
Can I enter decimals? Yes — both distance and speed accept decimal values like 12.5 km or 65.5 mph.
What does the arrival time mean? It is calculated by adding the travel duration to the current time, assuming you start immediately.
Does the swap button change my input values? No — it only switches the display units. Your original inputs remain unchanged.
